reasoning: HARSH AUDIT across 11 dims: (1) Outline weight — peers use tight 1-2px dark pixel outlines; candidate rope has comparable outline weight, marginal pass. (2) Outline color — peers use near-black/dark-gray outlines; candidate broadly matches, pass. (3) Shadow direction — peers show consistent top-left key light with bottom-right shadow on all helmet forms; candidate rope coil shows an interior radial highlight with a blown-out white center, implying a completely different lighting model (backlit or emissive), FAIL. (4) Shadow count — peers have one primary shadow zone; candidate has ambiguous layering inside the coil torus, marginal. (5) Highlight intensity — peers have restrained specular glints; candidate has a near-pure-white blown-out highlight occupying ~30% of the sprite area, far more intense than any peer, FAIL. (6) Color temperature — ALL peers are cool-toned: silver-gray, blue-steel, teal, with gold accents only on specific helmets; candidate is warm brown/tan/rust/orange throughout — zero cool-metal tones, FAIL. (7) Detail density — peers pack moderate pixel detail; candidate coil has comparable density, pass. (8) Proportion — candidate scale matches sprite grid, pass. (9) Rendering style — both are pixel art, broadly consistent, pass. (10) Palette overlap — peers share a cool-gray metallic palette; candidate introduces a completely separate warm earth-tone palette with negligible overlap (<5% shared hues), FAIL. (11) Charm level — peer helmets have personality and cute proportions characteristic of Denzi-style chibi equipment; the rope coil reads as utilitarian and flat in affect, lacking the expressive silhouette energy of the peer set, FAIL. Summary: 5 of 11 dimensions fail. The color temperature and palette divergence alone are disqualifying for a same-family sprite sheet.
